Scenario.title: Operation Honeybee: Disruption of Water Treatment Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple A sophisticated spear-phishing campaign targeting water treatment operators leads to exploitation of unpatched SCADA systems, resulting in operational disruption through process manipulation.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Application Log Content (Mechanism), Process Metadata (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends meticulously crafted phishing emails impersonating regulatory agencies, embedding malicious links that steal login credentials from employees accessing internet-facing SCADA monitoring portals.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, the attacker exploits a known vulnerability in the SCADA system's public-facing log analysis tool, injecting malicious payloads into log entries to execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-67: String Format Overflow in syslog()
stage.description: The attacker manipulates process metadata to alter parameters of critical water treatment processes, such as pump speed and chemical dosing rates, causing operational disruptions and potential service outages.

Scenario.summary: A multi-stage attack leveraging phishing, application exploitation, and process manipulation compromises water treatment infrastructure, demonstrating the vulnerability of industrial control systems to targeted cyber operations.
